description: Second in a two-year series of integrated math courses for AET students exploring relationships between trigonometry, conic sections, polar graphing, parametric equations, vectors, and calculus concepts. Students have the opportunity to take the AP Precalculus Exam for possible college credit.
subject: Math

title: AET AP Calculus AB
description: Explores limits, continuity, derivatives, and integrals using verbal, numerical, analytical, and graphical approaches. Applications include rates of change, optimization, curve analysis, and integrals as Riemann sums. Students have the opportunity to take the AP Calculus AB Exam in May for possible college credit.
subject: Math

title: AET AP Calculus BC
description: Rigorous treatment of calculus topics including parametric, polar, and vector functions, advanced integration techniques, sequences and series, and applications extending into physics and computer science. Students have the opportunity to take the AP Calculus BC Exam for possible college credit.
subject: Math

title: AET AP Statistics
description: Covers AP Statistics topics including data collection and interpretation, binomial and normal distributions, probability, linear correlation and regression, analysis of variance, and descriptive statistical methods. Students have the opportunity to take the AP Statistics Exam in May for possible college credit.
subject: Math

title: AET Multivariable Calculus
description: Covers differential, integral, and vector calculus for functions of more than one variable. Includes methods for computing double and triple integrals and the theorems of Gauss, Green, and Stokes. The tool Mathematica is integrated throughout the course.
subject: Math

title: AET Integrated Science I
description: First in a two-year series of integrated science courses for AET students covering physics and chemistry as an integrated progression. Inquiry-based with content primarily learned through laboratory exercises. Required for all AET Engineering and Entrepreneurship freshmen.
subject: Science

title: AET Integrated Science II
description: Second in a two-year series of integrated science courses for AET students covering physics and chemistry as an integrated progression. Inquiry-based with content primarily learned through laboratory exercises. Required for all AET Engineering and Entrepreneurship sophomores.
subject: Science

title: AET Physics
description: Rigorous inquiry-based physics course for AET IT pathway students covering mechanics, electricity and magnetism, and waves and optics. Incorporates algebraic and trigonometric concepts alongside laboratory work including graphical analysis.
subject: Science

title: AET Research Biology
description: Problem-solving and inquiry-based biology course covering biochemical life processes, cellular organization, mechanisms of inheritance, dynamic relationships among organisms, and changes in organisms through time. Incorporates mathematical analysis, computational thinking, and engineering design experiences.
subject: Science

title: AET Research Chemistry
description: Rigorous chemistry course for AET IT pathway sophomores emphasizing scientific research methodology to develop chemistry content knowledge. Students collect and communicate data using descriptive statistics and graphical representations, and develop independent research questions and investigation designs.
subject: Science

title: AET AP Applied Advanced Computer Science II
description: Second in a two-year series of computer science courses for AET IT pathway students completing the AP Computer Science curriculum. Topics include sorting algorithms, data structures such as arrays, linked lists, stacks, queues, trees, and graphs, and programming in Java. Students have the opportunity to take the AP Computer Science Exam in May for possible college credit.
subject: Math

title: AET AP Physics
description: College-level AP Physics course emphasizing mechanics across six content areas: kinematics, Newton's laws, work/energy/power, systems of particles and linear momentum, circular motion and rotation, and oscillations and gravitation. Pre-calculus and calculus skills are applied throughout. Students have the opportunity to take the AP Physics Exam in May for possible college credit.
subject: Science

title: AET Engineering Studies III, Junior Research
description: Eleventh grade engineering course building on AET Engineering I and II through problem-based labs in Aerospace, Civil, Electrical, and Biomedical engineering disciplines. Emphasizes microcontroller programming, prototyping, biomedical design, and robotics, with training in planning and implementing a research project.
subject: Career and Technical Education

title: AET Computer Science Investigations, Junior Research
description: Eleventh grade IT elective building on prior computer science coursework to explore Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, and Mobile/Web Application Development. Includes training in planning and implementing a research project. Counts for either a math or science credit.
subject: Math

title: AET AP Biology
description: College-level AP Biology course covering evolution, cellular processes, energy and communication, genetics, information transfer, ecology, and integrations. Emphasizes inquiry-based laboratory investigations. Students have the opportunity to take the AP Biology Exam in May for possible college credit.
subject: Science

title: AET AP Environmental Science
description: College-level AP Environmental Science course including fieldwork and extended lab procedures. Covers population biology, ecosystems, earth systems, atmospheric science, land and water use, energy, pollution, and global change. Students have the opportunity to take the AP Environmental Science Exam in May for possible college credit.
subject: Science

title: AET AP Chemistry
description: College-level AP Chemistry course with many extended lab procedures. Introduces organic chemistry, biochemistry, nuclear chemistry, coordination complexes, and semi-micro qualitative analysis. Students have the opportunity to take the AP Chemistry Exam in May for possible college credit.
subject: Science

title: AET Senior Research - Computer Systems
description: Interdisciplinary science research course using inquiry-based methodology to develop research and development techniques and scientific writing at a publication level. Students continue a research project developed in junior research under the guidance of a faculty mentor with regular presentation of results.
subject: Math

title: AET Senior Research - Engineering
description: Culminating fourth-year course for AET Engineering students conducting a year-long interdisciplinary engineering research project using inquiry-based methodology. Students present results through notebook documentation, poster creation, and verbal presentations.
subject: Career and Technical Education

title: AET Applied Advanced Computer Science I
description: First in a series of computer science courses for AET IT pathway students covering object-oriented programming, graphics and animation, GUI interfaces, arrays and files, and ethics. Programming languages include Java, Python, and Arduino C. Students design and conduct multiple programming and research projects.
subject: Math

title: AET Engineering Studies I
description: First in a series of engineering studies courses for AET Engineering students covering the engineering design process through inquiry-based, problem-based exercises. Students apply mathematical and scientific concepts to hands-on engineering projects and communicate findings through presentations, proposals, and technical reports.
subject: Career and Technical Education

title: AET Engineering Studies II
description: Second in a series of engineering studies courses for AET Engineering students emphasizing integration of mathematics and science into engineering problems. Students practice engineering skills and communication of technical information while applying the engineering design process to multiple projects.
subject: Career and Technical Education

title: AET Entrepreneurship I
description: First in a series of Entrepreneurship courses for AET students covering business ownership, economics, finance, customer service, sales, and marketing with a STEM focus. Students develop multiple STEM-focused business and marketing plans.
subject: Career and Technical Education

title: AET Entrepreneurship II DE
description: Second in a series of Entrepreneurship courses for AET students focusing on advanced entrepreneurship strategies in STEM industries. Builds on Entrepreneurship I with emphasis on developing a start-up business plan using Lean Methodologies from initial concept to final investor pitch.
subject: Career and Technical Education

title: AOS Pre-Integrated Math
description: Honors-level alternative to Geometry for highly motivated mathematics students covering plane, solid, and coordinate geometric concepts. Includes investigations of lines, planes, congruence, similarity, areas, volumes, circles, and three-dimensional shapes. Emphasizes reasoning, problem solving, and formal proof including two-column, paragraph, and coordinate proofs.
subject: Math

title: AOS Integrated Math I
description: First in a two-year series of integrated math courses for AOS students covering geometry, advanced algebra, trigonometry, math analysis, and AP Calculus A topics. Emphasizes mathematical communication, reasoning, authentic problem solving, and multiple representations. Mathematical concepts are connected to AOS Integrated Science courses.
subject: Math

title: AOS AP Integrated Math II
description: Continuation of AOS Integrated Math I covering elementary, transcendental, and logistic functions, parametric, polar, matrix, and linear algebra systems, infinite sequences and series, differential calculus, and an introduction to integration. Includes inferential statistics topics connected to independent science research.
subject: Math

title: AOS AP Calculus AB
description: Covers all AP Calculus AB topics from the College Board including use of differential equations to model dynamic processes in physical and biological sciences. Students have the opportunity to take the AP Calculus AB Exam in May for possible college credit.
subject: Math

title: AOS AP Calculus BC
description: Covers all AP Calculus BC topics from the College Board including use of differential equations to model dynamic processes in physical and biological sciences. Students have the opportunity to take the AP Calculus BC Exam in May for possible college credit.
subject: Math

title: AOS Multivariable Calculus
description: Vector calculus course emphasizing vector fields to model motion of particles and fluids in two and three dimensions. Includes double and triple integrals and the theorems of Gauss, Green, and Stokes to measure flow and turbulence. Mathematica is used throughout to create interactive graphics.
subject: Math

title: AOS Integrated Science I
description: First in a series of integrated science courses for AOS students covering physics, chemistry, and earth science as an integrated progression. Inquiry-based with many student-designed laboratory exercises. Students take the Earth Science and Chemistry SOL tests during the course progression. Required for all AOS 9th graders.
subject: Science

title: AOS Integrated Science II
description: Second in a series of integrated science courses for AOS students covering physics, chemistry, and earth science as an integrated progression. Inquiry-based with many student-designed laboratory exercises. Students take the Earth Science and Chemistry SOL tests during the course progression. Required for all AOS 9th graders.
subject: Science

title: AOS Integrated Science III
description: Third in a series of integrated science courses for AOS students covering physics, chemistry, and earth science as an integrated progression. Inquiry-based with many student-designed laboratory exercises. Students take the Earth Science and Chemistry SOL tests during the course progression. Required for all AOS 10th graders.
subject: Science

title: AOS Sophomore Science Research
description: Interdisciplinary science research course involving students in inquiry-based methodology, laboratory techniques, and scientific writing at a publication level. During the second semester, students begin an independent science research project of their own design. Required for all AOS 10th graders.
subject: Science

title: AOS Junior Science Research
description: Continuation of interdisciplinary science research using inquiry-based methodology with increased laboratory skill development. Students develop an independent research plan including literature search, laboratory protocols, materials list, and budget under a faculty mentor with regular presentation of results.
subject: Science

title: AOS Senior Science Research
description: Culminating interdisciplinary science research course using inquiry-based methodology. Students conduct bench-work on a plan developed in junior research under the guidance of a faculty mentor, with regular presentation of results. Some students interact with local scientist mentors during the school year and summer.
subject: Science

title: AOS AP Biology
description: College-level AP Biology course covering cellular biology, biochemical processes, vertebrate anatomy and physiology, advanced genetics, evolution, plant anatomy and physiology, and ecology. Students have the opportunity to take the AP Biology Exam in May for possible college credit.
subject: Science

title: AOS AP Environmental Science
description: College-level AP Environmental Science course including fieldwork and extended lab procedures. Covers population biology, ecosystems, geologic and earth science concepts, atmospheric science, land and water use, energy, pollution, and global change. Students have the opportunity to take the AP Environmental Science Exam in May for possible college credit.
subject: Science

title: AOS AP Chemistry
description: College-level AP Chemistry course with many extended lab procedures introducing organic chemistry, biochemistry, nuclear chemistry, coordination complexes, and semi-micro qualitative analysis. Students have the opportunity to take the AP Chemistry Exam in May for possible college credit.
subject: Science

title: AOS AP Physics
description: College-level AP Physics course emphasizing mechanics with optional additional topics. Pre-calculus and calculus skills are used to develop concepts and solve problems. Students have the opportunity to take the AP Physics Exam in May for possible college credit.
subject: Science

title: MATA Administration of Justice I SEM H
description: First semester course in the MATA Administration of Justice pathway preparing students for entry-level employment in law enforcement and criminal justice. Topics include criminal justice professions, ethics, criminal and civil law, use of force, patrol functions, community policing, criminal investigation, search and seizure, crime scene investigation, and the juvenile justice system.
subject: MATA

title: MATA Administration of Justice II SEM H
description: Second semester course in the MATA Administration of Justice sequence. Covers constitutional law, substantive and procedural law, interviews and interrogations, search and arrest warrants, organized crime, federal task forces, complex criminal investigation, international terrorism, counterterrorism strategies, and advanced crime scene investigation.
subject: MATA

title: MATA Auto Collision Repair Technology I SEM
description: First semester course in the MATA Auto Collision Repair Technology sequence providing basic knowledge of automobile construction and skills in body shop materials, equipment, tools, and procedures. Covers basic welding, dent and plastic repair, bolt-on parts replacement, and estimating.
subject: MATA

title: MATA Auto Collision Repair Technology II SEM
description: Second semester course in the MATA Auto Collision Repair Technology sequence. Covers preparing vehicles for painting, mixing and applying automotive paints, identifying and removing paint defects, and paint detailing. Explores career options including collision repair technician, refinish technician, estimator, and insurance adjuster.
subject: MATA

title: MATA Auto Servicing Technology I SEM
description: First semester course in the MATA Auto Servicing Technology sequence providing training in brakes, steering and suspension, engine repair, and HVAC using ASE and NATEF industry-standard curriculum. Students may obtain ASE certification and participate in internship programs with local dealerships.
subject: MATA

title: MATA Auto Servicing Technology II SEM
description: Second semester course in the MATA Auto Servicing Technology sequence covering electricity, manual drivetrain and axle, engine performance, and automatic transmission using ASE and NATEF curriculum. Students may obtain ASE certification and participate in internship programs with local dealerships.
subject: MATA

title: MATA Auto Servicing Technology III
description: Third course in the MATA Auto Servicing Technology sequence covering the full second year of instruction in electricity, manual drivetrain and axle, engine performance, and automatic transmission. Students may obtain ASE certification and participate in internship programs with local dealerships.
subject: MATA

title: MATA Biomedical Technology I DE
description: First year of a two-year pathway covering biomedical techniques including bioprocessing, DNA analysis, medicine, biomechanical systems, and environmental applications. Students are introduced to laboratory procedures supporting bioscience research and investigate safety, quality assurance, and ethical concerns in biomedical technology.
subject: MATA

title: MATA Biomedical Technology II
description: Second year of the MATA Biomedical Technology pathway focusing on human body systems, disease prevention, diagnosis, and treatment. Students take on roles of biomedical professionals to solve real-world medical cases and develop a research project addressing health challenges or compete in HOSA.
subject: MATA

title: MATA Biotechnology
description: One-year course introducing biotechnology including medical, industrial, agricultural, marine, and bioinformatics applications. Topics include plant-based pharmaceuticals, bioethics, forensics, health and medical applications, and environmental management. Students use laboratory equipment and complete an independent research project.
subject: MATA

title: MATA Building Construction I SEM
description: First semester course in the MATA Building Construction sequence covering residential construction and carpentry. Includes OSHA certification, structural components, foundation work, floor and wall framing, interior and exterior finishes, and cabinetry construction and installation.
subject: MATA

title: MATA Building Construction II SEM
description: Second semester course in the MATA Building Construction sequence with advanced skills and independent work. Students earn their OSHA 10 Card Certification and receive professional accreditation from NCCER and SkillsUSA Carpentry certification upon graduation.
subject: MATA

title: MATA CISCO I DE QTR
description: First-quarter course covering fundamentals of computer and mobile device hardware, software, networking, and security. Students learn to assemble a computer system, install operating systems, connect devices to the Internet, and share resources in a networked environment. Prepares students for the CompTIA A+ certification exam.
subject: MATA

title: MATA CISCO II DE QTR
description: Second-quarter course building on CISCO I using Cisco Networking Academy CCNAv7 curriculum. Students learn how networks operate, build simple LANs, configure routers and switches, and implement IP. Prepares students toward the Cisco CCNA certification exam.
subject: MATA

title: MATA CISCO III DE QTR
description: Third-quarter course building on CISCO I and II covering enterprise and theoretical networking models, router and switch architecture, wireless LANs, and network security. Students configure and troubleshoot routers and switches using security best practices for IPv4/IPv6 networks.
subject: MATA

title: MATA CISCO IV DE QTR
description: Final course in the CISCO sequence covering architecture, components, operations, and security for large complex networks including WAN technologies, network virtualization, and automation. Prepares students to take the Cisco CCNA certification exam.
subject: MATA

title: MATA Computer & Digital Animation I H
description: First year of the MATA Computer & Digital Animation sequence introducing 2D animation through graphics, design concepts, storyboarding, motion graphics, animation principles, and environmental geometry. Software includes Adobe Photoshop, Illustrator, InDesign, After Effects, Animate, Toon Boom Harmony, and 3dsMax.
subject: MATA

title: MATA Computer & Digital Animation II
description: Second year of the MATA Computer & Digital Animation sequence introducing 3D animation through object manipulation, texturing, lighting, and environmental geometry. Students participate in the school film festival and learn production skills including proposals and budgets. Software includes Adobe Creative Cloud, Toon Boom, 3dsMax, and Blender.
subject: MATA

title: MATA Computer Integrated Engineering & Design (CIED) I H
description: First-year CIED course providing foundational skills in manufacturing and engineering design through hands-on, task-based activities. Students explore engineering disciplines including Aerospace, Civil, Electrical, and Mechanical, and are introduced to 2D and 3D CAD/CAM software, 3D printing, laser cutting, CNC milling, and robotics.
subject: MATA

title: MATA Computer Integrated Engineering & Design (CIED) II H
description: Second-year CIED course extending foundational topics from CIED I with development of proficiency in CAD/CAM software and digital fabrication. Students develop a substantial engineering design-related project throughout the course.
subject: MATA

title: MATA Cosmetology I SEM
description: First semester of the MATA Cosmetology sequence introducing shampooing, hairstyling, scalp treatments, hair cutting, chemical relaxing, permanent waving, hair coloring, facials, make-up, wig care, and manicuring. Related subjects include public health, personal hygiene, bacteriology, and salon management.
subject: MATA

title: MATA Cosmetology II SEM
description: Second semester of the MATA Cosmetology sequence developing job-entry skills for a professional salon environment. Covers advanced hair cutting, chemical texture services, hair coloring, artificial nail applications, waxing, salon management, and anatomy and physiology of the scalp, skin, and nails.
subject: MATA

title: MATA Cosmetology III
description: Full second year of the MATA Cosmetology sequence building proficiency in hair cutting and styling on live models, chemical processes, advanced hair coloring, skin and nail care including facials, manicures, pedicures, and nail enhancements. Prepares students for the Virginia State Licensing Exam.
subject: MATA

title: MATA Culinary Arts I SEM
description: First semester of the MATA Culinary Arts sequence preparing students for the culinary industry. Covers safety, sanitation, equipment use, recipe calculation, food storage and preparation, and skills in preparing appetizers, soups, salads, meats, breads, and desserts. Includes catering events and culinary competitions.
subject: MATA

title: MATA Culinary Arts II SEM
description: Second semester of the MATA Culinary Arts sequence covering advanced production and service skills. Topics include nutrition, marketing, sustainability, ethnic cuisines, baking techniques, menu planning, costing, and inventory control. Students complete Workplace Readiness Testing and the ServSafe Industry test.
subject: MATA

title: MATA Cybersecurity I DE SEM
description: First semester of the MATA Cybersecurity program covering computer hardware assembly, operating system installation and configuration, computer troubleshooting, and networking fundamentals. Students may qualify to take the CompTIA A+ certification exam upon successful completion.
subject: MATA

title: MATA Cybersecurity II DE SEM
description: Second semester of the MATA Cybersecurity program developing advanced skills in computer networking and resource sharing. Students may qualify to take the CompTIA A+, Network+, and/or Security+ certification exams upon successful completion.
subject: MATA

title: MATA Emergency Medical Technician (EMT) I DE SEM
description: First semester EMT course providing training to function in a medical emergency as a link in the health care team. Covers emergency medical care for traumatic injury, CPR, basic patient assessment, vital signs, and Mass Casualty Incident Command.
subject: MATA

title: MATA Emergency Medical Technician (EMT) II DE SEM
description: Second EMT course building on EMT I covering EMS operations, medical emergencies, and management of special patient populations. Requires supervised field experience outside school hours. Successful completion may lead to eligibility for the Virginia State Psychomotor Exam and National Registry EMT cognitive exam.
subject: MATA

title: MATA Emergency Medical Technician (EMT) III DE SEM
description: Third EMT course continuing topics from EMT I and II covering EMS operations, medical emergencies, and management of special patient populations. Requires supervised field experience outside school hours. Successful completion may lead to eligibility for the Virginia State Psychomotor Exam and National Registry EMT cognitive exam.
subject: MATA

title: MATA Environmental Plant Science I DE SEM
description: First semester of a plant science pathway focused on environmental responsibility. Topics include horticulture, forestry, natural resource management, plant botany and physiology, sustainable landscaping, woody plants, soil science, and business practices. Industry certifications available through Virginia Nursery and Landscape Association and others.
subject: MATA

title: MATA Environmental Plant Sciences II DE SEM
description: Second semester of the MATA Environmental Plant Science pathway covering plant propagation and genetics, fertilization practices, plant growth and development, greenhouse systems, plant diagnostics and pest management, and sustainable agricultural practices.
subject: MATA

title: MATA Graphic Communications I
description: First course in the MATA Graphic Communications sequence introducing graphic design, computer graphics, and visual communication. Students work individually and in teams on digital projects covering layout, typography, image manipulation, illustration, animation, and web design. Students may obtain PrintEd certifications.
subject: MATA

title: MATA Graphic Communications II
description: Second course in the MATA Graphic Communications sequence covering advanced design concepts, portfolio development, contracts, copyright, freelance business practices, and client-based work. Students run a creative design firm, work with printers, and may obtain PrintEd certifications.
subject: MATA

title: MATA Health Informatics
description: One-year pathway exploring health information technology including the history of health IT, medical terminology, Electronic Health Records, ethical and privacy issues, cybersecurity and data breaches, clinical decision support, and emerging healthcare technologies. Includes hands-on simulation of an EHR program.
subject: MATA

title: MATA Heating, Ventilation, & Air Conditioning (HVAC) I SEM
description: First semester of the MATA HVAC sequence providing basic knowledge and skills in residential and commercial air conditioning, refrigeration, and heating. Topics include basic electricity, safety, refrigeration physics, soldering, electrical components, wiring diagrams, plumbing, sheet metal fabrication, and duct installation.
subject: MATA

title: MATA Heating, Ventilation, & Air Conditioning (HVAC) II SEM
description: Second semester of the MATA HVAC sequence providing advanced skills in installing, maintaining, and repairing heating and cooling equipment. Covers air distribution, refrigeration equipment, oil, gas, electric and heat pump systems, and plumbing. Students may qualify for EPA certification and HVAC Excellence exams.
subject: MATA

title: MATA Introduction to Health and Medical Sciences DE
description: One-year course introducing a broad range of health and medical profession careers. Covers medical terminology, pharmacology, anatomy and physiology, therapeutic and diagnostic interventions, and professional and ethical issues. Students rotate through various medical settings for observational experiences.
subject: MATA

title: MATA Masonry I SEM
description: First course in the MATA Masonry sequence familiarizing students with materials, tools, equipment, and practices of the masonry trade. Students develop skills in laying concrete block, brick, and ceramic tile, and study blueprint reading and labor and material estimates. Students are eligible to obtain an OSHA-10 certificate.
subject: MATA

title: MATA Masonry II SEM
description: Second course in the MATA Masonry sequence advancing skills with concrete, block, brick, and ceramic tile through projects such as chimneys, fireplaces, sidewalks, and arches. Includes repair and renovation work and stone masonry for walls, fireplaces, and entryways.
subject: MATA

title: MATA Medical Laboratory Technology I DE
description: First-year course in Medical Laboratory Technology providing foundational knowledge and skills for medical laboratory science careers. Covers clinical chemistry, urinalysis, microscopy, clinical hematology, and phlebotomy through laboratory exercises reflecting clinical settings. Students may participate in organized field experiences.
subject: MATA

title: MATA Medical Laboratory Technology II DE
description: Second-year course building on Medical Laboratory Technology I covering clinical microbiology, immunohematology, coagulation and hemostasis, and clinical laboratory biotechnology. Laboratory exercises reflect routine clinical settings and students may participate in organized field experiences.
subject: MATA

title: MATA Nurse Aide I SEM DE
description: First semester of the MATA Nurse Aide pathway covering growth and development, anatomy and physiology, medical terminology, infection prevention, and entry-level nursing skills including vital signs and CPR. Includes a required forty-hour clinical rotation at a long-term care facility. Prepares students for the NNAAP exam.
subject: MATA

title: MATA Nurse Aide II SEM DE
description: Second semester of the MATA Nurse Aide sequence building on Nurse Aide I. Includes a required forty-hour clinical rotation at a long-term care facility. Students successfully completing the course take the NNAAP exam to become a Certified Nurse Aide.
subject: MATA

title: MATA Pharmacy Technology I H
description: First year of a pharmacy technician certificate program providing basic skills and knowledge to begin work as a pharmacy technician. Coursework fulfills Virginia Board of Pharmacy requirements and prepares students for state or national Pharmacy Technician Certification Board exams. Includes shadowing and organized field experiences.
subject: MATA

title: MATA Pharmacy Technology II H
description: Second year of the pharmacy technician certificate program fulfilling Virginia Board of Pharmacy requirements and preparing students for state or national Pharmacy Technician Certification Board exams. Emphasizes clinical field experiences and coordinated work experiences.
subject: MATA

title: MATA Radiology Technology I DE
description: First-year radiology technology course covering the radiographer's role in the health care team, anatomy and physiology for diagnostic imaging, safe use of radiation, history of the radiological profession, medical ethics, radiobiology, radiation protection, and the future of radiologic technology careers.
subject: MATA

title: MATA Radiology Technology II H
description: Second-year radiology technology course building on Radiology Technology I. Covers anatomy and physiology, disease processes, physics of medical imaging equipment, radiation protection, patient care in radiology, vital signs assessment, immobilization techniques, sterile techniques, and management of medical emergencies.
subject: MATA

title: MATA Television Production & Digital Moviemaking I SEM
description: First semester course introducing scriptwriting, storyboarding, camera operation, non-linear editing, lighting, audio production, and studio control room operations for single-camera productions. Students create an ePortfolio including an individually produced television commercial.
subject: MATA

title: MATA Television Production & Digital Moviemaking II SEM
description: Second semester course building toward complex multicamera media productions. Students write, plan, direct, mix, and edit multicamera studio productions from the control room, adding a completed live in-studio television segment to their ePortfolio.
subject: MATA

title: MATA Veterinary Science I SEM
description: First semester of the MATA Veterinary Science program covering anatomy and physiology, medical terminology, safety and sanitation, parasitology, animal nutrition, breeds, and professional conduct. Students perform clinical exams and laboratory procedures coordinated with area veterinary clinics.
subject: MATA

title: MATA Veterinary Science II SEM
description: Second semester of the MATA Veterinary Science program expanding knowledge in animal structure and function, disease prevention, parasitology, and genetics. Students develop advanced skills in first aid, surgery, aseptic techniques, medication administration, and office functions with potential clinical instruction at veterinary offices.
subject: MATA

title: MATA Welding I DE SEM
description: First semester welding course covering Oxy-fuel Welding and Cutting, Plasma Arc cutting, Shielded Metal Arc, and beginning GMAW welding processes. Includes art and ornamental welding and preparation for the American Welding Society (AWS) Test.
subject: MATA

title: MATA Welding II DE SEM
description: Second semester welding course emphasizing Shielded Metal Arc Welding in horizontal, all-position, and pipe configurations. Students may practice for the AWS Limited and Unlimited Welding Thickness Test and pursue welding certification. Includes advanced MIG and TIG process work.
subject: MATA

title: MATA Welding III DE
description: Capstone welding course covering emerging industry technologies and Gas Tungsten Arc Welding (GTAW) and Shielded Metal Arc Welding (SMAW) pipe tests. Prepares students to earn industry credentials toward employment in production or manufacturing facilities.
subject: MATA
